chain.thought: Explanation: We look for the earliest year where a golfer’s first championship win is not their first overall WGC win. Geoff Ogilvy won the Championship in 2008, and he had already won the Match Play in 2006. Therefore, 2008 is the first year a seasoned competitor achieved their initial championship title.

Explanation: We interpret “initial title” as the golfer’s first win in the Championship event, and “not their initial title” as the golfer already having won a different WGC event before that Championship win. From the sub-table, Geoff Ogilvy (row 2) won the Championship in 2008 for the first time, and he had previously won Match Play in 2006. That makes him a seasoned competitor whose Championship win was his initial title in that event but not his initial WGC title overall. Among all listed years satisfying this, 2008 is the earliest.

judge_thought:
Explanation:
The condition implies the first year someone won their initial Championship title while already having a prior win (i.e., seasoned, not their initial overall title). Checking Championship entries:
- 1999 (Tiger Woods): first Championship title, but no prior wins listed before 1999, so not seasoned by the table.
- 2004 (Ernie Els): first Championship title, no prior wins, so not seasoned.
- 2008 (Geoff Ogilvy): first Championship title (1: 2008), and he previously won Match Play in 2006, so he is seasoned.
No earlier year meets this condition. Therefore, 2008 is the first year that fits.
